source_jobs.title: [Rolling] Short-Form Video Editor for @JohnnyChangLive & @UnlearnedWisdom
source_jobs.description:
We hire for this role on a rolling basis. We always want strong applications on file, even when we're not running an active process. Standout applications get flagged for the next opening. Everything else gets archived. If your application doesn't follow the instructions below or doesn't show real proof of the work, we won't respond. If you've got the receipts to prove you can do this at the level we need, drop your application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - Johnny Chang YouTube: @johnnychanglive https://www.youtube.com/@johnnychanglive

 - Johnny Chang Instagram: @johnnychanglive https://www.instagram.com/johnnychanglive

 - Unlearned Wisdom YouTube: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

 - Unlearned Wisdom Instagram: @unlearnedwisdom https://www.instagram.com/unlearnedwisdom

Location: Remote
Type: Contract
Department: Content




Your Role

A 30-second clip is often the very first time someone encounters the gospel. Someone scrolling TikTok at 2am, struggling with anxiety or addiction, stops on one of your edits. For the first time, they hear truth that changes their life. You are the person who crafts that moment. That is the weight and the privilege of this role.

You own the short-form pipeline end to end. You ship 20-30 videos per month across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts, pulling from long-form source content and turning it into clips that stop the scroll and drive real growth.




What You’ll Own

 - High-volume short-form editing: 20-30 polished clips per month, consistently on time and on brand

 - Storytelling from raw footage: find the moment, build the arc, make it land in under 60 seconds

 - Creative enhancement: motion graphics, kinetic text, music selection, and sound design that elevate every clip

 - Content mining: dig through the full library to surface evergreen moments and underused footage worth reshaping

 - Trend analysis: stay ahead of platform formats, sounds, and hooks that drive reach on TikTok, Reels, and Shorts

 - Performance analysis: review clip-level data, identify what is working, and feed those insights back into your edits

 - Creative ideation: bring concepts and angles to the table, not just execution
   



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Deliver viral-ready clips that grow our reach

 - Contribute to hitting 75M-100M+ monthly views across platforms

 - Average views per clip: 300K-400K is good, 500K-750K is great, 1M+ is beast mode

Objective 2: Execute with speed and precision

 - 100% on-time delivery rate for all final edits

 - Less than 3 revision rounds per video after the first month
   



This Role Is NOT For You If:

 - You cannot point to clips you have personally edited that broke 1M+ views

 - You need heavy direction to find the story inside raw footage

 - You treat short-form as a lesser format rather than its own craft

 - You edit to finish, not to stop the scroll

 - High volume without quality dropoff is not something you have proven you can sustain

 - You are unfamiliar with how TikTok, Reels, and Shorts each reward different formats and pacing
   



Non-Negotiable Requirements

 - A portfolio of viral short-form work with real view counts attached

 - Proven ability to build compelling stories from raw, unscripted footage

 - Deep platform knowledge: TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts are not interchangeable to you

 - Expert-level Adobe Premiere Pro

 - Proficiency in ClickUp, Frame.io, and Google Drive as daily production tools
   



Compensation

Phase 1 (60 days): $50-$100 per video based on skill level, plus performance bonuses.

Phase 2: Monthly retainer of $2,000-$4,000 plus bonuses based on output and performance.


How to Apply

 1. Resume

 2. Send us clear proof of clips you’ve edited

 3. Little video of why you’re the right fit. Keep it under three minutes.
    



Our

source_jobs.title: [Rolling] YouTube Long-Form Video Editor (Vlogs, Docu-series, etc) for @JohnnyChangLive
source_jobs.description:
We hire for this role on a rolling basis. We always want strong applications on file, even when we're not running an active process. Standout applications get flagged for the next opening. Everything else gets archived. If your application doesn't follow the instructions below or doesn't show real proof of the work, we won't respond. If you've got the receipts to prove you can do this at the level we need, drop your application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - Johnny Chang YouTube: @johnnychanglive https://www.youtube.com/@johnnychanglive

 - Johnny Chang Instagram: @johnnychanglive https://www.instagram.com/johnnychanglive

 - Unlearned Wisdom: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

Location: Remote (Los Angeles bonus)
Type: Contract
Department: Content




Your Role

Every vlog and docu-series episode you cut is someone’s first 15 to 30 minutes inside Johnny’s world. They are not watching a highlight reel. They are watching the gospel lived out in real time: the tension of a hard conversation, the weight of a mission-driven decision, the ordinary moments that point to something bigger. Your edit is the thing that either holds them or loses them. This is not a vanity project. It is a front-line discipleship tool, and the editor determines whether the story lands.

You own the full post-production pipeline for Johnny’s long-form YouTube content, from raw footage to published video. You work inside a structured system using ClickUp and Google Drive, hit deadlines without hand-holding, and bring a creative point of view that serves the story rather than showing off.




What You’ll Own

 - Edit 4-8 long-form YouTube videos per month (vlogs, docu-series, interviews) from raw footage to final export, on time, every time

 - Build and maintain a repeatable workflow inside ClickUp and Google Drive so nothing falls through the cracks

 - Make narrative decisions that serve retention: structure episodes so viewers stay, not just start. Own your AVD numbers and know why they move

 - Deliver color-corrected, mixed, and fully formatted videos with thumbnail assets and chapter markers ready for upload

 - Bring one creative experiment per quarter with a clear hypothesis, execute it, and report results with data

 - Flag problems early. If a deliverable is at risk, communicate before the deadline, not after

 - Stay current on YouTube algorithm shifts, format trends, and editing tools and bring relevant insights to the team
   



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Own the delivery pipeline

 - 100% on-time delivery rate on all assigned videos with zero missed deadlines by Day 30

Objective 2: Move the needle on performance

 - Baseline Average View Duration (AVD) in Week 1. Target 15% improvement by Day 90

Objective 3: Own the creative direction

 - Execute at least 1 creative experiment with measurable results reported

 - Workflow fully documented and running without oversight by Day 90
   



This Role Is NOT For You If:

 - You have never edited YouTube videos that consistently crossed 500K+ views

 - You treat deadlines as suggestions or need repeated follow-up to close out a deliverable

 - You edit to show off your style instead of serving the story and the audience

 - You are not comfortable working in a faith-driven environment where the mission shapes every content decision

 - You do not track retention data or care about why viewers drop off
   



Non-Negotiable Requirements

 - 4+ years editing narrative, vlog, or docu-style content specifically for YouTube

 - Portfolio that includes long-form videos with verifiable performance (link to videos with visible view counts and retention context)

 - Expert-level Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, and Audition

 - Working knowledge of YouTube analytics: AVD, CTR, impression-to-watch ratio, and how edits influence each

 - Profici

source_jobs.title: [Talent Pool] Social Media Growth Strategist for @JohnnyChangLive & @UnlearnedWisdom
source_jobs.description:
This is not an active hiring process. We're collecting applications from people we'd want to talk to when a role like this opens. Standouts get held and reviewed against future openings. Applications that don't show real proof of the work get archived. If you're patient and want to be on our radar when the door opens, send us your strongest application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - Johnny Chang YouTube: @johnnychanglive https://www.youtube.com/@johnnychanglive

 - Johnny Chang Instagram: @johnnychanglive https://www.instagram.com/johnnychanglive

 - Unlearned Wisdom YouTube: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

 - Unlearned Wisdom Instagram: @unlearnedwisdom https://www.instagram.com/unlearnedwisdom

 - TikTok: @theejohnnychang https://www.tiktok.com/@theejohnnychang

Location: Remote (Los Angeles preferred)
Type: Contract
Department: Marketing




Your Role

Every new follower, every viral clip, every email subscriber is a person who just took their first step toward hearing the gospel. The teenager who discovers Johnny on TikTok and starts watching every day. The mom who sees a Reel, joins the email list, and six months later is in a daily Bible reading habit. You are the person who builds the systems and strategies that make those first encounters happen at scale.

You own growth strategy across all platforms, run a continuous experimentation engine, convert viewers into community members, and project manage content output so the team hits every deadline without dropping quality.




What You’ll Own

 - Growth strategy and execution across YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and email, setting goals, building the roadmap, and driving results week over week

 - Creative direction for viral content, collaborating with the team to identify hooks, formats, and topics that travel far and land with the right audience

 - Performance analysis with weekly reports tracking views, followers, email subscribers, and engagement rates across every platform

 - A/B testing engine: running continuous experiments on thumbnails, titles, captions, posting times, and content formats, then applying what works

 - Funnel building: converting casual viewers into email subscribers, Discord members, and app users through strategic calls to action and landing pages

 - Content scheduling and project management: owning the content calendar and managing the editing team to ensure every piece ships on time

 - Trend and platform research: staying ahead of algorithm shifts, emerging formats, and cultural moments we can use to reach new audiences
   



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Launch experiments and report results

 - Launch 5-10 content experiments across platforms with a full analytics report documenting what worked, what did not, and what to do next

Objective 2: Achieve measurable audience growth

 - Double monthly view count across short-form platforms. Exceptional: double follower acquisition rate

Objective 3: Own content operations

 - 100% on-time content scheduling for the full 90-day period

 - Project manage the editing team to hit every deadline with zero missed publish dates
   



This Role Is NOT For You If:

 - You have never grown a page beyond 100,000 followers on your own

 - You need someone else to set your priorities. This role requires a self-directed operator who builds systems, not someone who follows checklists

 - You do not have verifiable proof of results. Links, screenshots, and data are required, not optional

 - You are not comfortable working in a fast-moving, faith-based organization where the mission shapes every decision
   



Non-Negotiable Requirements

 - Portfolio of social accounts grown with verifiable proof (analytics screenshots, case studies, or live links)

 - 3-5+ years of digital marketing experience focused specifically on social media growth

 - Ex

source_jobs.title: [Rolling] Podcast Long-Form Video Editor for @UnlearnedWisdom
source_jobs.description:
We hire for this role on a rolling basis. We always want strong applications on file, even when we're not running an active process. Standout applications get flagged for the next opening. Everything else gets archived. If your application doesn't follow the instructions below or doesn't show real proof of the work, we won't respond. If you've got the receipts to prove you can do this at the level we need, drop your application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - YouTube: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

 - Instagram: @unlearnedwisdom https://www.instagram.com/unlearnedwisdom

Location: Remote
Type: Contract
Department: Content




Your Role

Every episode of Unlearned Wisdom is a conversation that could change someone’s life. Someone going through addiction, anxiety, depression, or spiritual confusion hits play, and for the next hour they hear a message that meets them exactly where they are. You are the person who makes sure that experience is so compelling they can’t look away, so polished they trust it, and so well-paced they stay until the final word.

You own the entire long-form post-production process for 1-2 podcast episodes per week, from raw multi-cam recordings to final publish-ready video on YouTube. You are not a task-doer waiting for instructions. You are the creative lead for how these episodes look, sound, and feel. You report to the Head of Content and collaborate closely with the team, but you run your edit bay like it’s your business.




What You’ll Own

 - Edit 1 video episode per week from multi-cam assembly through final delivery (story editing, color grading, sound mixing, B-roll, motion graphics)

 - Edit 1 audio-only episode per week (assembly, sound mixing, mastering, and final delivery)

 - Build and refine the visual storytelling language of every episode: identify the most powerful moments, structure the edit for emotional impact, and maximize audience retention

 - Design and iterate on brand insertions for internal initiatives and external partners

 - Review YouTube Studio retention graphs for every episode you cut and bring data-backed insights to your next edit

 - Organize all project files, raw footage, and assets in ClickUp and Google Drive so handoffs and archiving are seamless

 - Proactively pitch at least 1 new editing technique, graphic style, or creative experiment per month
   



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Master the workflow

 - Deliver first-draft edits within 72 hours of receiving footage and final revisions within 24 hours of feedback, starting after a 2-week ramp-up

 - Hit 100% on-time delivery for all assigned episodes by Day 30

Objective 2: Move the needle on performance

 - Baseline average view duration (AVD) in Week 1. Target a 15% increase in AVD on new episodes by Day 90. Exceptional: 25%+

Objective 3: Own the creative direction

 - Execute 1 new creative experiment (intro hook, graphic style, B-roll approach) that measurably improves an episode’s performance

 - Iterate and improve brand insertions for both internal and partner placements
   



This Role Is NOT For You If:

 - You have never edited long-form podcast episodes that performed well on YouTube. Adjacent video experience (wedding, corporate, music videos) does not qualify. Do not apply.

 - You edit to a timeline, not to a retention curve. If you’ve never studied a YouTube retention graph and changed your edit because of what you saw, this isn’t the role.

 - You wait for creative direction instead of bringing it. We need someone who shows up with ideas, not someone who asks “what do you want me to do?”

 - You treat audio as an afterthought. If your mixes have hiss, uneven levels, or audible cuts, you’re not ready.

 - You need daily check-ins to stay on track. This is a remote role with firm deadlines and no hand-holding.
   



Non-Negotiable

source_jobs.title: [Talent Pool] Technical Podcast Producer for @UnlearnedWisdom Podcast
source_jobs.description:
This is not an active hiring process. We're collecting applications from people we'd want to talk to when a role like this opens. Standouts get held and reviewed against future openings. Applications that don't show real proof of the work get archived. If you're patient and want to be on our radar when the door opens, send us your strongest application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - YouTube: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

 - Instagram: @unlearnedwisdom https://www.instagram.com/unlearnedwisdom

 - YouTube: @johnnychanglive https://www.youtube.com/@johnnychanglive

 - Instagram: @johnnychanglive https://www.instagram.com/johnnychanglive

Location: Los Angeles, CA (in-studio Monday, Tuesday, and Saturday every week)
Type: Contract
Department: Content




Your Role

Every recording session is the moment the gospel gets captured. When Johnny sits down and speaks truth into someone’s addiction, anxiety, or spiritual confusion, you are the person making sure that moment is technically flawless so nothing gets between the message and the person who needs to hear it. A corrupted file, a missed angle, a bad audio mix doesn’t just cost us an episode. It costs someone their encounter with God.

You own the entire production pipeline for the Unlearned Wisdom podcast, from in-studio setup and recording through asset handoff and post-production coordination to final quality sign-off before publish. You are not just a camera operator. You are the technical backbone of everything this podcast produces. You run the studio, manage the post-production workflow, and own the data that tells us what’s working and what’s not.




What You’ll Own

 - Run all in-studio technical production (multi-cam, audio, lighting, livestreaming) for 2-3 recording sessions per week

 - Operate the ATEM Mini Pro ISO and RØDECaster Pro II during all sessions, ensuring every file is captured without error

 - Deliver all footage, timestamped editing notes, and project assets to the post-production team within 10 hours of recording

 - Project manage the full post-production workflow: track editing progress, unblock editors, and ensure every episode hits its release date

 - Own final quality sign-off on all long-form and short-form content before publish (edit quality, caption accuracy, brand alignment)

 - Pull performance data weekly from YouTube Studio and propose at least 1 optimization per month to improve retention or engagement
   



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Zero technical failures

 - 0 instances of lost footage, corrupted audio, or technical errors due to setup or operational mistakes across all recording sessions

 - 100% of assets organized and delivered to post-production within 10 hours of recording, every session

Objective 2: Own the post-production pipeline

 - 100% of episodes published on schedule with no missed release dates

 - Establish a documented production checklist (pre-shoot, recording, handoff, QC) reviewed and approved by the team by Day 30
   



This Role Is NOT For You If:

 - You have never produced a video podcast from setup through publish. Studio camera work, live event production, or film production does not qualify. Do not apply.

 - You can’t troubleshoot technical problems calmly under pressure. If a camera goes down mid-record and you freeze, this isn’t the role.

 - You see production as your only job. This role includes post-production project management and performance analysis. If you only want to be in the studio, it’s not the right fit.

 - You’ve never used YouTube analytics to inform a production decision. We need a producer who thinks about what the data says, not just what looks good on set.

 - You need to be managed daily. This is a high-autonomy role with firm deadlines.
   



Non-Negotiable Requirements

 - Must be based in Los Angeles

 - Avail

source_jobs.title: [Now Hiring] Live Events Producer for @JohnnyChangLive
source_jobs.description:
We're actively hiring for this role right now. Our team is reviewing applications as they come in. Strong applications get a response. Weak ones, and applications that ignore the instructions below, get archived without a reply. Send us your strongest proof of the work.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.

Check out our content before applying:

 - Johnny Chang YouTube: @johnnychanglive https://www.youtube.com/@johnnychanglive

 - Johnny Chang Instagram: @johnnychanglive https://www.instagram.com/johnnychanglive

 - Unlearned Wisdom Podcast: @UnlearnedWisdom https://www.youtube.com/@UnlearnedWisdom

References of our live events:

 - San Diego https://www.instagram.com/p/DLn50HvyZZB/

 - New York https://www.instagram.com/p/DHriah8SsFL/

 - Beach Meet-up https://www.instagram.com/p/DNWoOoIS_fQ/?img_index=1

 - Whittier https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uwKyHuuTLro&t=271s

Location: Must be US-based. Los Angeles preferred. Open to remote.
Type: Contract (Part-Time)
Department: Live Events




YOUR ROLE

Every live event we produce is a chance for someone to encounter God for the first time. The person in the back row of a church who almost didn't come. The family that drove two hours because they saw a clip online. The teenager who's been watching Johnny's videos alone in their room and finally gets to be in a room full of people who believe the same thing. You are the person who makes that moment happen, flawlessly, every single time.

As our Live Events Producer, you own the entire live event lifecycle for Johnny Chang Live, from initial outreach and venue booking through on-the-ground production and post-event wrap-up. You will plan and execute everything from local church gatherings to multi-city tours to prison ministry visits. You work directly with leadership, coordinate vendors and volunteers, and build the systems that let us scale our in-person mission globally. You are not an assistant. You are the operator who runs this arm of the ministry.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Own all event and tour logistics: routing, scheduling, venue booking, travel, and accommodations for the entire team.

 - Lead all pre-production for every event, including tech riders, vendor contracts, run-of-show documents, and volunteer coordination.

 - Run on-site production, managing vendors, partners, AV, and volunteers to deliver a flawless attendee experience from doors-open to load-out.

 - Build and maintain a global pipeline of partner churches, venues, and event contacts to keep future bookings flowing without leadership having to source them.

 - Own all event-related communications (internal and external), keeping ClickUp updated with 100% clarity on every event's status, timeline, and open items.

 - Manage post-event wrap-up: vendor payments, debrief notes, attendee follow-up coordination, and documentation of what worked and what didn't.

 - Create and refine repeatable systems (checklists, templates, vendor databases) so every event runs at the same standard regardless of city or scale.
   
    




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (FIRST 90 DAYS)

Objective 1: Book and begin production for our entire 2026 and 2027 live event calendar.

Our current pace is roughly one weekend of live events per month. You'll have clear systems to follow from Day 1.

 - Book and successfully complete at least 3 weekends of live events in your first 90 days.

 - Deliver a complete pre-production package (tech rider, run-of-show, travel plan, vendor contracts) for each event at least 30 days out.

Objective 2: Build a scalable outreach pipeline for future bookings.

 - Create a polished "Johnny Chang Live Event" partnership package and send it to at least 15 high-potential churches or venues.

 - Generate at least 5 qualified booking conversations from that outreach by Day 90.

Objective 3: Prove operational ownership inside our systems.

 - Every event you touch has a fully maintained ClickUp project wi

source_jobs.title: [Talent Pool] Content Specialist for Johnny Chang & Unlearned Wisdom podcast
source_jobs.description:
This is not an active hiring process. We're collecting applications from people we'd want to talk to when a role like this opens. Standouts get held and reviewed against future openings. Applications that don't show real proof of the work get archived. If you're patient and want to be on our radar when the door opens, send us your strongest application.



Our Mission: Bring biblical clarity and daily guidance to people seeking God in a chaotic world.


Before you apply, watch our content:

 - Instagram: @johnnychanglive & @unlearnedwisdom

 - YouTube: @johnnychanglive & @unlearnedwisdom

 - TikTok: @theejohnnychang

Location: Remote
Type: Contract
Contract Hours: 20-25 hours/month
Reports to: Devon Hess, Head of Content
Tools: Frame.io http://Frame.io, ClickUp, Discord



The Role

Every piece of content COTH publishes is someone's first encounter with the gospel. A 30-second video might be the thing that stops someone mid-scroll and starts them on a path toward building a real relationship with God. You are the person who makes sure that moment hits as hard as it possibly can.

You are the quality filter between our editors and our audience. You review every short-form video before it goes live and give specific, tactical feedback that elevates it. Not "this needs work." You tell the editor exactly what to fix: the hook is too slow, the text placement blocks the subject at 0:03, the punchline lands at :42 but retention drops at :35 so move it up. Your eye for detail has a direct line to someone's spiritual life. Prove yourself on short-form, and this role expands into long-form video review with increased comp.



What You'll Own

 - Review ~40 short-form videos/month in Frame.io http://Frame.io with timestamped, specific feedback on hooks, pacing, storytelling, text placement, audio, and scroll-stop moments. 90% of your time is here.

 - Manage your review queue within two-week sprint cycles, ensuring every video gets 1-2 revision rounds before publish

 - Track content performance across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts and translate data into actionable recommendations ("this hook style averages 2x the retention of our standard openings, let's use it more")

 - Surface 2-3 new format or content ideas per month based on what's trending in short-form across platforms (not just faith content, any viral mechanics COTH can adapt)

 - Collaborate directly with editors and Devin via Discord and ClickUp to keep feedback loops tight and deadlines hit

 - Have direct access to platform analytics and a seat in monthly content strategy sessions so your feedback is always data-informed
   
   
   

Who You Are

 - You care about the mission. You understand that a well-edited 30-second video isn't just content. It might be the reason someone opens a Bible for the first time.

 - You think in retention curves, not vibes. When you watch a video, you instinctively clock why the hook works (or doesn't), where attention drops, and what would fix it.

 - You're obsessed with short-form content across platforms, not just faith content. You study what's working everywhere and reverse-engineer why.

 - You manage yourself. You don't need someone checking your queue. You track your own deadlines, flag problems early, and keep things moving without being asked.



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days)
Objective 1: Own the Review Process

 - Complete your first full sprint cycle (20 video reviews with revisions) within first 14 days

 - Hit 100% on-time review completion across all sprint cycles by Day 30

 - Establish a personal review workflow and feedback template, reviewed and approved by Devin, by Day 21

Objective 2: Make the Content Better

 - Baseline our average view count in Week 1. Target a 15% increase in average views per video by Day 90.

 - At least 3 videos where your feedback demonstrably changed the final edit outperform our 30-day rolling average by 25%+

Objective 3: Start Thinking Ahead

 - Deliver at least 2 new
